summary: Completed comprehensive testing of SuperNetworkAI after PostgreSQL migration. Fixed critical /api/profile/completeness route ordering bug. All 17 backend API tests passed. Frontend UI fully functional with dark mode design. PostgreSQL + pgvector semantic search working correctly.

resolved_bugs.issue: FastAPI route ordering bug - /profile/{profile_id} was matching 'completeness' as a profile_id

passed_tests: PostgreSQL database accessible with 10 seeded profiles
Session-based authentication via cookies and Bearer tokens
POST /api/search - pgvector semantic search with LLM re-ranking
POST /api/search with role_filter - correctly filters by role
Landing page loads with SuperNetworkAI branding
Dashboard displays 3 AI-matched profiles with insights
Profile page shows 100% completeness and Ikigai sections
Search page returns ranked results with AI explanations
Messages page shows split-pane conversation layout
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: Unused variables in server.py (lines 661, 986) should be cleaned up
Consider adding health check endpoint /api/health for monitoring
seed_data_creation: Created test user 'test_user_pg_001' with profile and ikigai in PostgreSQL
context_for_next_testing_agent: PostgreSQL migration complete and working. Test session token: test_session_pg_001. Database has 10 seeded profiles + 1 test profile. The /api/profile/completeness route was fixed - ensure it stays before /profile/{profile_id} route.
rca_of_the_issue: The /api/profile/completeness endpoint was returning 404 because FastAPI routes are matched in order of definition. The dynamic route /profile/{profile_id} at line 420 was matching 'completeness' as a profile_id before the specific /profile/completeness route at line 926. Fix was to move the specific route before the dynamic one.
